The conservation stewardship program under subpart B of part II of subchapter IV (as in effect on the day before ), using such sums as are necessary to administer contracts entered into before .December 20, 2018December 20, 2018

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: b Availability of funds
children.text: Amounts made available by subsection (a) for fiscal years 2014 through 2031 shall be used by the Secretary to carry out the programs specified in such subsection and shall remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be available for the provision of technical assistance for the programs for which funds are made available as necessary to implement the programs effectively;

children.label: B
children.text: except for technical assistance for the conservation reserve program under subpart B of part I of subchapter IV, shall be apportioned for the provision of technical assistance in the amount determined by the Secretary, at the sole discretion of the Secretary; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall not be available for the provision of technical assistance for conservation programs specified in subsection (a) other than the program for which the funds were made available.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the delivery of technical assistance under the Soil Conservation and Domestic Allotment Act ( et seq.), the Secretary shall give priority to producers who request technical assistance from the Secretary in order to comply for the first time with the requirements of subchapter II and subchapter III of this chapter as a result of the amendments made by section 2611 of the Agricultural Act of 2014.16 U.S.C. 590a

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report regarding the extent to which the conservation compliance requirements contained in the amendments made by section 2611 of the Agricultural Act of 2014 apply to and impact specialty crop growers, including national analysis and surveys to determine the extent of specialty crop acreage that includes highly erodible land and wetlands.February 7, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: detailing the amount of technical assistance funds requested and apportioned in each program specified in subsection (a) during the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: any other data relating to this provision that would be helpful to such Committees.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the extent to which the requests for highly erodible land conservation and wetland compliance determinations are being addressed in a timely manner;

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of requests completed in the previous fiscal year;

children.label: C
children.text: the incomplete determinations on record; and

children.label: D
children.text: the number of requests that are still outstanding more than 1 year since the date on which the requests were received from the producer.

children.heading: Relationship to other law
children.label: d Relationship to other law
children.text: The use of Commodity Credit Corporation funds under subsection (c) to provide technical assistance shall not be considered an allotment or fund transfer from the Commodity Credit Corporation for purposes of the limit on expenditures for technical assistance imposed by .section 714i of title 15

children.heading: Equitable distribution
children.label: 1 Equitable distribution
children.text: When determining funding allocations each fiscal year, the Secretary shall, after considering available funding and program demand in each State, provide a distribution of funds for conservation programs under subchapter IV (excluding the conservation reserve program under subpart B of part I), subchapter VII, and subchapter VIII to ensure equitable program participation proportional to historical funding allocations and usage by all States.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that during the first quarter of each fiscal year each State has the opportunity to establish that the State can use an aggregate allocation amount of at least 0.6 percent of the funds made available for those conservation programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: for each State that can so establish, provide an aggregate amount of at least 0.6 percent of the funds made available for those conservation programs.

children.heading: Acceptance and use of contributions for public-private partnerships
children.label: f Acceptance and use of contributions for public-private partnerships

children.heading: Establishment of public-private partnership contributions accounts
children.label: 1 Establishment of public-private partnership contributions accounts
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the necessary accounts and process to accept contributions of private funds for the purposes of addressing the changing climate, sequestering carbon, improving wildlife habitat, protecting sources of drinking water, and addressing other natural resource priorities identified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Deposit and use of contributions
children.label: 2 Deposit and use of contributions
children.text: Contributions of non-Federal funds received for a covered program shall be deposited into the account established under this subsection for the program and shall be available to the Secretary, without further appropriation and until expended, to carry out the program.

children.label: i
children.text: the source of the funds to be contributed;

children.label: ii
children.text: the natural resource concerns to be addressed through the use of the funds;

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of funds to be contributed;

children.label: iv
children.text: whether the activities proposed to be carried out using the funds are consistent with the priorities of the Secretary; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other factors the Secretary determines to be relevant.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: B Determination
children.text: A determination of whether to accept private funds under this subsection shall be at the sole discretion of the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary may provide matching Federal funds, and determine the level of such match, which shall not exceed 75 percent, for the private funds contributed under this subsection, subject to the availability of funding for the applicable covered program.

children.heading: Distribution of Federal funding for States
children.label: B Distribution of Federal funding for States
children.text: The Secretary may not provide any matching Federal funds pursuant to subparagraph (A) in a manner that would result in a substantial reduction in the historical distribution of Federal funding to any State for any covered program.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: No funds made available pursuant to may be used to provide matching Federal funds pursuant to subparagraph (A).Public Law 117–169

children.label: A
children.text: designate the covered program for which the contributed funds are intended to be used;

children.label: B
children.text: specify the geographic area in which the contributed funds are intended to be used;

children.label: C
children.text: identify a natural resource concern the contributed funds are intended to be used to address;

children.label: D
children.text: with respect to an activity funded pursuant to this subsection that may result in environmental services benefits to be sold through an environmental services market, subject to the approval of the Secretary, prescribe the terms for ownership of the entity’s share of such environmental services benefits resulting from such activity; and

children.label: E
children.text: work with the Secretary to promote the activities funded pursuant to this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: of activities that may be carried out, through a covered program, pursuant to this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any terms prescribed by the contributing entity under paragraph (5)(D) with respect to such activities.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “of”.1

children.heading: Retention of environmental services benefits
children.label: B Retention of environmental services benefits
children.text: The Secretary shall not claim or impede any action of a producer with respect to the environmental services benefits they accrue through activities funded pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall ensure that the terms and conditions of activities carried out using funds contributed under this subsection are consistent with the requirements of the applicable covered program.

children.label: I
children.text: to provide a simplified process; or

children.label: II
children.text: to better reflect unique local circumstances and to address a specific priority of the contributing entity.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not adjust the application of statutory requirements for a covered program, including requirements governing appeals, payment limits, and conservation compliance.

children.label: A
children.text: the name and a description of each entity contributing private funds under this subsection that took an action under paragraph (5), and a description of each such action;

children.label: B
children.text: the name and a description of each entity contributing private funds under this subsection for which the Secretary has provided matching Federal funds, and the level of that match, including the amount of such matching Federal funds; and

children.label: i
children.text: private funds contributed under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: matching Federal funds provided by the Secretary under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: subchapter IV (except for subpart B of such subchapter), subchapter VII, or subchapter VIII;2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: B
children.text: ;section 2203 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: title V of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 ( et seq.); or16 U.S.C. 6571

children.label: D
children.text: the Watershed Protection and Flood Prevention Act ( et seq.), except for any program established by the Secretary to carry out section 14 of such Act ().16 U.S.C. 100116 U.S.C. 1012

children.heading: Duration of authority
children.label: 10 Duration of authority
children.text: The authority of the Secretary under this subsection shall expire, with respect to each covered program, on the date on which the authority of the covered program expires.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 1 Review
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary, acting through the Chief of the Natural Resources Conservation Service and the Administrator of the Farm Service Agency, shall conduct a review of conservation programs and authorities under this chapter that utilize annual allocation formulas to determine the sufficiency of the formulas in accounting for relevant data on local natural resource concerns, resource inventories, evaluations and reports, recommendations from State technical committees established under , State-level economic factors, level of agricultural infrastructure, or related factors that affect conservation program costs.December 20, 2018section 3861(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the formulas adequately reflect the costs of carrying out the conservation programs;

children.label: B
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, local natural resource concerns are considered a leading factor in determining annual funding allocation to States;

children.label: C
children.text: the process used at the national level to evaluate State budget proposals and to allocate funds is reviewed annually to assess the effect of allocations in addressing identified natural resource priorities and objectives; and

children.label: D
children.text: the allocation of funds to States addresses priority natural resource concerns and objectives.

children.heading: Assistance to certain farmers or ranchers for conservation access
children.label: h Assistance to certain farmers or ranchers for conservation access
i
children.text: 5 percent to assist beginning farmers or ranchers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 5 percent to assist socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers.

children.label: i
children.text: 5 percent to assist beginning farmers or ranchers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 5 percent to assist socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers.

children.heading: Repooling of funds
children.label: 2 Repooling of funds
children.text: In any fiscal year, amounts not obligated under paragraph (1) by a date determined by the Secretary shall be available for payments and technical assistance to all persons eligible for payments or technical assistance in that fiscal year under the environmental quality incentives program and, in the case of fiscal years 2019 through 2031, under the conservation stewardship program under subpart B of part IV of subchapter IV.

children.heading: Repooling of acres
children.label: 3 Repooling of acres
children.text: In any fiscal year through fiscal year 2018, acres not obligated under paragraph (1)(A) by a date determined by the Secretary shall be available for use in that fiscal year under the conservation stewardship program.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: 4 Preference
children.text: In providing assistance under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall give preference to a veteran farmer or rancher (as defined in section 2279(e) of title 7) that qualifies under, as applicable, clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(A) or clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(B).2

children.heading: Report on program enrollments and assistance
children.label: i Report on program enrollments and assistance

children.text: The annual and current cumulative activity reflecting active agreement and contract enrollment statistics.

children.label: A
children.text: payments made under the agricultural conservation easement program for easements valued at $250,000 or greater;

children.label: B
children.text: payments made under the regional conservation partnership program subject to the waiver of adjusted gross income limitations pursuant to ;section 3871c(c)(3) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: waivers granted by the Secretary under ;section 1308–3a(b)(3) of title 7

children.label: D
children.text: exceptions and activity associated with ; andsection 3839aa–2(h)(2) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: exceptions provided by the Secretary under .section 3865b(b)(2)(B)(ii) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: standards for conservation practices under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: technical guidelines for implementing conservation practices under this chapter, including the location of the conservation practices; and

children.label: C
children.text: standards for conservation plans.

children.heading: Consistency of farm service agency technical standards and payment rates
children.label: 2 Consistency of farm service agency technical standards and payment rates
A
children.text: technical standards of programs administered by the Farm Service Agency are consistent with the technical standards established by the Natural Resources Conservation Service under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: payment rates, to the extent practicable, are consistent between the Farm Service Agency and the Natural Resources Conservation Service.
